Problems may require using information from tables, graphs or Venn diagrams (limited to two sets). 2 Calculate the probability of a single event. 3 Understand that the probability of an event not occurring = 1 – the probability of the event occurring.

Calculate with frequency density. Frequency density is defined as frequency density = frequency ÷ class width. Faculty feedback: ‘Understanding how and why our climate is changing and providing the knowledge and skills to explore the challenges plays a key role in every student’s education.’ Feedback from: Dr Amy Munro-Faure, Head of Education and Student Engagement of Cambridge Zero
